blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: His heart. Couldn't it have been his heart?

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: He ran. He ate clean. He went to the doctor every year, I know he did because he always texted me his results like I was his personal physician.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: He'd been off the medication for two years. He told me.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Maybe he went back on it. Maybe he didn't tell you.
He didn't sound convinced. He sounded like someone trying to build a house out of materials that kept shifting.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: It doesn't matter what it was. The point is the door was locked and the window is impossible. Which means whatever happened, happened to him alone. Nobody else was in that room.
She said it quietly. Still looking at her mug. Everyone turned to look at her.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: We know the door was locked. We know the window looks impossible. That's not the same as knowing nobody was in that room.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Nadia — how? How would someone get in and out?

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: I don't know. I'm just saying we don't know.
The word hung there. Nobody picked it up.
